zh.lifeSummary: 當Elinor Holland於1254年出生在英國藍卡爾武普 霍蘭時，她的父親Sir Thurstan de Holand33歲，母親de Kellet27歲。她於1274年與Robert Cherleton在英國史洛普爾結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1328年在英國藍卡爾享年74歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Elinor Holland于1254年出生在英国藍卡爾武普 霍蘭时，她的父亲Sir Thurstan de Holand33岁，母亲de Kellet27岁。她于1274年与Robert Cherleton在英国史洛普爾结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1328年在英国藍卡爾享年74岁。

nameMeanings.name: Holland
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, German, Dutch, Danish, Norwegian, Swedish, French, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): habitational name from Holland, a province of the Netherlands.

English: habitational name from Downholland or Upholland (Lancashire), Hulland (Derbyshire), the Parts of Holland, one of the three administrative subdivisions of Lincolnshire, any of the four places called Hoyland (southern Yorkshire), and possibly Great and Little Holland (Essex). The placenames all derive from Old English hōh ‘heel, spur of land’ + land ‘land’.

English: habitational name either from Hoeland (Farm) in Bury (Sussex), or from Holland's Barn in Albourne (Sussex). The placename in Bury has the same etymology as in 1 above, while the placename in Albourne may derive from Old English hol ‘hole, hollow’ + land ‘land’.

variants.name: Hollands
meanings: English: variant of Holland , especially 3, with post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Howland
meanings: History: Howland was the name of three Quaker brothers, original settlers in Marshfield, MA. They were from Huntingdonshire, England. The eldest, John Howland (c. 1593–1672) was a passenger on the Mayflower, servant to Gov. John Carver, who died in the first winter at Plymouth Colony.

variants.name: Hollander
meanings: Dutch (also Den Hollander), German (also Holländer), English, Swedish, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): habitational name for someone from Holland (see Holland 1).

variants.name: Holand
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Holland  .
Norwegian: habitational name from about twenty farmsteads so named, composed of Old Norse hár ‘high’ + land ‘land, field’, denoting a high-lying farm.

variants.name: Aland
meanings: Scandinavian (Åland): topographic name from the Åland Islands in the Gulf of Bothnia.

English: topographic name from Middle English atte lande (Old French laund) ‘dweller at the glade or pasture’, or sometimes perhaps, ‘dweller at the old cultivated land(s)’ from Old English eald, ald ‘old’ + land ‘land’.

nameMeanings.name: Elinor
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Eleanor
meanings: From an Old French respelling of the Old Provençal name Alienor. This has sometimes been taken as a derivative of Helen , but it is more probably of Germanic derivation (the first element being ali ‘other, foreign’ the second is obscure). The name was introduced to England by Eleanor of Aquitaine ( 1122–1204 ), who came from Aquitaine in south-west France to be the wife of King Henry II . It was also borne by Eleanor of Provence, the wife of Henry III , and Eleanor of Castile, wife of Edward I .

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

en.lifeSummary: When Elinor Holland was born in 1254, in Up Holland, Lancashire, England, her father, Sir Thurstan de Holand, was 33 and her mother, de Kellet, was 27. She married Robert Cherleton in 1274, in Shropshire, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 1 daughter. She died in 1328, in Lancashire, England, at the age of 74.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Elinor Holland est née en 1254, à Up Holland, Lancashire, Angleterre, son père, Sir Thurstan de Holand, avait 33 ans et sa mère, de Kellet, avait 27 ans. Elle a épousé Robert Cherleton en 1274, à Shropshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée en 1328, à Lancashire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 74 ans.

ja.lifeSummary: Elinor Hollandが1254年にイギリスランカシュアイアウップ ホーランドで生まれた時，父Sir Thurstan de Holandは33歳，母de Kelletは27歳であった。1274年にイギリスシュロプシュアイアでRobert Cherletonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1328年にイギリスランカシュアイアで74歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Elinor Holland(이)가 1254년 영국 랭카실르 업 홀랜드에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Thurstan de Holand(은)는 33세였고, 그녀의 어머니 de Kellet(은)는 27세였다. 그녀는 1274년 영국 슈럽실르에서 Robert Cherleton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1328년 영국 랭카실르에서 74세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elinor Holland родилась в 1254 г. в местности уп холланд, ланкашир, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir Thurstan de Holand было 33 года, а матери, которую звали de Kellet, 27. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Robert Cherleton в 1274 г. в местности шропшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла в 1328 г. в местности ланкашир, Англия в возрасте 74 лет.
